title: Seeker's Stone-Carving Expedition
summary: Seeker, a young dwarf living in a vast mountain kingdom, is sent by his mother Ovage on an expedition to learn the secrets of his people. He first visits a tunnel-digging crew to understand the heavy labor and tools required to shape their underground world. His journey then takes him to the Great Library, where he observes master carvers creating a beautiful stone image of a cat named Silver on the ceiling. Inspired, Seeker visits a workshop to carve his own masterpiece—a stone finger—which he proudly displays in his home. After a day of exploration, Seeker returns to his family for a feast of beef and sweet potatoes before settling in for bed.

highlightQuote.text: I can dig tunnels with my fingernails and hide special acorns in there and cover it the holes up with dirt.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeping you out.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeping you out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeping you out out out.
Now what kind of stars and stories would you like?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I would like a Seeker story, Seeker story, Seeker story, Seeker's time to get telling.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, so Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeker's time to get doing things. We better do some things.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, turn back on the light.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, why?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because I need a diaper on.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well we're just doing stars and stories, you're not going to sleep yet.
I need a diaper on.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Do you need to go potty?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No.
No? You just want your diaper?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Well, no, not really.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well I don't want you to pee in your bed, so what do you choose: diaper or no diaper?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Diaper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's put it on.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But diaper is after stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Usually it is, that's true.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad! Dad, I changed my mind to... to do diaper after stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Well, I just got it out, so it's ready whenever you need it. All right. Turn off the light. Boop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now it's time to get doing and telling and telling and doing things.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Tell do, tell do.
Now, let's tell a story about an adventure that Seeker had when he was just a little boy. And Seeker is a special kind of creature called a dwarf.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: What's a dwarf?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A dwarf? Dwarfs are small people that live inside of tunnels in the stone deep underneath the mountains. And they're very good at cutting and carving the stone. They love to make things out of stone.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they love to make wood out of stone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, they make every place that they live. They cut tunnels into the rock underneath the mountains, and parts of the tunnels have big chambers, which are their houses that they live in, and even bigger places where they can get together and have a feast or a party or a meeting. And there's big long hallways, and libraries, and restaurants.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Tell! Tell! This isn't really telling.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're right. Tell, you're supposed to tell. So the dwarven kingdom high in the mountains, in the tunnels underneath the mountain, was where Seeker grew up. And when he was a little boy, he was very curious about places that he wasn't supposed to go. Because...
And seek—and Dad, you're supposed to be telling about Seeker and Seeker's mom.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yeah, what was Seeker's mom's name?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Ovage.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Ovage. Well, Seeker's mom, Ovage, was a very interesting person. She was very creative.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And did she say anything to Seeker?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: She said, 'Seeker, I would like you to learn about, uh, how we dig tunnels through the mountain. So I'm going to send you out on an expedition to a place on the other side of our kingdom where a group of dwarfs is digging a tunnel, and you can learn about it and come back and tell me what you learned. How's that sound?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah. 'I'll ask them that Seeker's coming. Hey guys, digging a big tunnel please.'
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And they said, 'Yes.'
'Um, is it okay if Seeker comes to watch what you're doing?'
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: 'Why yes, of course, we would love to teach you. We love to have a little dwarf around to explain how we do things around here. And maybe when you grow up, you can dig tunnels too.'
'I can dig tunnels with my fingernails and hide special acorns in there and cover it—the holes up with dirt.'
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Ah, but here—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'And I can also plant seeds and cover them up with dirt.'

'I see,' said Seeker. Or 'I see,' said the dwarf who was digging the tunnel. 'Well, here we're digging a tunnel through the mountain, and the mountain is made of stone, not dirt. So we can't dig through it with our hands, it's too hard. We have to use tools. What kind of tools do you think we use?'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Shovels.

Yes. Sometimes we use shovels, when the rock is loose, like piles of gravel. We use a shovel and we pick up some of the loose rocks and we put them in a little cart like this one right here.' And he pointed to a little cart with wheels and it was on a track, like a train, but it was little. And someone has to push the cart up the tracks all the way to a deep hole where we dump out the rocks and then they bring the cart back here and get more rocks. It's very hard work. And another kind of tool that we use is called a pick. It's a sharp heavy thing and a very strong dwarf swings it against the rock wall and then crack! It breaks the stone into little pieces. And then another dwarf has to scoop them up in his shovel and put them in a cart, and another dwarf has to bring the cart to the hole and dump them out. And so all day long it's just crack, crack, bang, bang, shovel, shovel, wheel, wheel, dump, dump. Crack, crack, bang, bang, shovel, shovel. And that's how we do it here. Watch.'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: And so the dwarfs got back to work, cracking the stone wall and breaking it apart to make more tunnel, and shoveling away the rocks and rolling them away on the cart. And Seeker sat there and watched and was amazed. And then he said, 'I have a question.' What was Seeker's question?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just keep telling the story, then you'll find out Seeker's question.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Seeker said, 'I have a question. I'm curious about carvings. Do you know about carvings?' And the tunnel-digging team said, 'Well, we just dig tunnels. We're not like those carving guys. You might want to go over to the Great Library. I heard that they're carving some new beautiful decorations there. You can learn from the carvers.' And Seeker said, 'Oh, okay. Well thank you for teaching me about how to make a tunnel.' And he headed over through another tunnel to the middle of the dwarven kingdom, and right in the middle of the dwarven kingdom was the Great Library.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'I'll ring the doorbell. Ding dong.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker rang the doorbell. And a dwarf who worked there, the dwarven librarian, came and opened the door and said, 'Ah, hello little one. What's your name?'
'Seeker.' And Seeker says, 'Um, I want to do carving and I also want to watch a bit first, and then do some carving and make a decoration to bring home.'
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: 'My my, you're ambitious for a little one! And I love your curiosity about the carving, and you're in luck because today a team of expert carvers has come to this library to add a new decoration to our ceiling. And they are carefully carving a whole set of pictures in the stone. Would you like to watch them now?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah. So Seeker benjed, benjed, benjed, joins them and she said, 'What are you doing with all those pictures?'
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And the team of carvers said, 'Oh, welcome little one! We'll show you how we carve. We use all of these tools.' And they were tools a little bit like the ones that the dwarfs carving the tunnel used, but they were much smaller, because these carvers were making art. They were carving pictures out of the stone.
And Seeker said, 'I want a decoration to bring home to show my mom and put up for Christmas.'
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: 'You'd like to make your own stone carving, little one?'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Well, we can teach you, but first, watch us make this little bit of carving that we're making.' What were they making a picture of on the ceiling of the library?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, they were making a picture of my cat Silver.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They were making a picture of a cat, carved from stone, high on the top of the ceiling of the Great Library. And so they said, 'Come with us, Seeker.' And they climbed up very tall ladders very carefully, and they said, 'Seeker, hold on tight while you watch.' And while they stood high on their ladders, they used their little tools, little picks and little scrapers and grinders and drills, and they banged and carved, and they sanded and smoothed the stone all around the edges of a big shape. And gradually, as they chipped away the stone in little pieces, one piece at a time, Seeker could start to see the shape take form. Oh, those are little ears sticking out the top! And then one of the carvers chipped away two little round spots, and Seeker said, 'Oh, I think those are the eyes!' And another carver was chipping away the stone around the bottom where the paws were, and carefully shaping each little claw. And another carver was making Silver's beautiful silky tail. And they said, 'As you can see, little Seeker, it's a lot of work making a big, complex picture like this out of stone. But it will last a very long time, and everyone who comes into the library can look up at the ceiling and see the beautiful picture of a cat and know that we love our library, because that's what dwarfs do, we love to learn. Do you love to learn, Seeker?'
Yes I do.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: 'Ah, well, we're done with our time carving for today, so let's climb down the ladder and we'll get a piece of stone and show you how to carve it.' And so they all climbed down the ladder together, and then they said, 'Let's go to our carving workshop.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And—and they went to the carving workshop and Seeker carved and she carved and she carved and she carved a picture of a finger.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A picture of a finger! Oh. Okay. And so she carved and she carved and she carved and then it was all done and she put—and she walked home and she put—and she showed it to her mom and she did more carving until it was done and she climbed up a ladder and put it on her ceiling.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker's mom said, 'Wow! You did so much more than I expected. You learned about tunnel making, but also about carving pictures, and you even carved your own. What a wonderful expedition you went on, Seeker.'
And then what did Seeker's mom say?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, she said, 'You've had a big day. I think it's time we sat down for dinner.' And what did they have for dinner?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: They had broccoli, beef, chicken, sweet potato, and broccoli and some ketchup.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And that was a good thing too. And milk and water.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And who else was in the family?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dada and the mom and the baby were named Felisa and Chove.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So Felisa was the Dada and Chove was the baby.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Baby Chove. And what kind of things does baby Chove do?
She chews.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: She chews on things. Yeah. And so they all had a delicious dinner and after dinner, you know what they did together? They had a little story time. And then it was bedtime and everyone went to sleep. Is that the end of our stories?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's the end of stars and stories.
I'll take off the basket... no Dad, don't turn off the light yet.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Okay. But don't look right at it. You want to make shadows?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's a little... bird eyes... a whale... okay.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Ovage
characters.description: Seeker's creative and interesting mother who encourages his curiosity and learning.

characters.name: The Librarian
characters.description: The dwarf who manages the Great Library and welcomes Seeker to watch the artisans work.

characters.name: The Carvers
characters.description: A team of expert artisans who create detailed stone art, including a carving of a cat on the library ceiling.

characters.name: Silver
characters.description: Immortalized in the stone of the Great Library yet reborn in Cat World as a small, hungry kitten, Izzy is a brave companion who now traverses the heart of Limbo. Communicating through an ancient, rhythmic language of resonant purrs and wielding a masterfully handled magical can opener, this tiny spirit balances her arcane legacy with a newfound, desperately urgent need for snuggles.

characters.name: Chove
characters.description: The baby in Seeker's family who is known for chewing on things.

places.name: The Mountain Kingdom
places.description: An underground realm deep beneath the mountains consisting of tunnels, chambers, houses, and restaurants.

places.name: The Great Library
places.description: The central hub of knowledge in the kingdom, featuring elaborate stone-carved decorations on its ceilings.

places.name: The Carving Workshop
places.description: A specialized room where dwarf artisans teach the craft of creating art from stone.
